title: Simplified mount cross-system change workflow
purpose: Model a late request that lets a player ride a mount and requires impact analysis across state, input, movement rules, and save data.

root_objective.title: Maintain a simple character movement system
root_objective.description: Keep player movement deterministic while allowing staged changes to movement features.
constraints: Walking movement must continue to work.
acceptance: Player position changes when walking input is applied.

objectives.title: Represent player position
objectives.description: Track player position as deterministic state.
acceptance: Position can be read before and after movement.

objectives.title: Apply walking input
objectives.description: Move the player by applying walking input to position.
constraints: Walking does not require mount state.

steps.request: Allow the player to ride a mount, move using mounted speed, dismount, and save the mounted state.

preserved_constraints: Walking movement must continue to work.
possibly_invalidated_acceptance: Walking-only movement acceptance may be incomplete once mounted movement exists.
unknowns: Mount collision and animation are deferred.
Network replication is not part of this slice.

acceptance: Player can mount, move at mounted speed, dismount, and reload mounted state.

regression_constraints: Walking movement still updates Position when not mounted.
Existing Position semantics do not change for unmounted players.

records.prompt: Manual graph run prompt: admit mounted movement only after cross-system impact analysis over Player, Mount, Position, input, movement rules, and save data.

notes: The request is too broad for direct implementation; first slice and regression constraints must enter active context.
Graph-level verification confirms cross-system impact analysis captures state, input, movement, and save-data effects.
